title: dactylorhiza pardalina
intro: Dactylorhiza pardalina, also known as the Marsh Helleborine, is a species of orchid that is native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its greenish-yellow flowers and fleshy, oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its saponin and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza pardalina are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or capsules, and can be found in traditional medicine and some modern herbal supplements.
